infra_and_schema.description: Infrastructure and Database Schema Layer (SQLAlchemy models and Docker Compose setup for Postgres)

frontend_atomic_design.description: Frontend React scaffold using atomic design principles, including base components, layout structure, and build configuration.

system_orchestration.description: System Entry Point and Orchestration, including Docker infrastructure and application main entry point.

frontend_optimistic_ui.description: Implement Optimistic UI logic, local request queue, and state reconciliation for the Task MVP frontend.

observability_resilience.description: Implement centralized logging, metrics (Prometheus-style), and health checks for the Task MVP.

ci_cd_testing_infra.description: CI/CD and Testing Infrastructure. Includes GitHub Actions pipeline for testing, linting, and building, as well as test framework setup (pytest, playwright).

backend_api.description: Backend API for Task CRUD operations, including FastAPI routes, request validation, and service layer logic.

api_integration.description: Backend service for API integration and reliable data fetching, providing a resilient interface for the task management system.

api_integration_refactor.description: Refactor API Integration layer for frontend integration, routing logs to stdout.

backend_api_integration.description: Refactoring and integrating backend API into the unified entry point and infrastructure. Ensuring all routes are /api/v1 prefix and integrated with logging middleware.

ci_cd_integration_tests.description: Integration testing for CI/CD infrastructure, verifying frontend-backend connectivity via container network.

system_orchestration_final_integration.description: I am managing the final system orchestration to ensure all services (Fastify API, React Frontend, Postgres DB) are correctly wired and bootable. I will update docker-compose.yml to include all services and create a working run script.

backend_api_crud_integration.description: Backend API Core CRUD Implementation and Integration according to Architecture Spec.

api_integration_finalization.description: API Integration, Data Fetching, and Frontend-Backend Sync. Implementing robust, type-safe API communication.

frontend_optimistic_ui_final.description: Optimistic UI Request Queue hardening, adding event emitter for status updates and retry mechanism. Replacing existing requestQueue.ts with robust implementation.

ci_cd_smoke_pipeline.description: Finalizing CI/CD infrastructure with smoke test pipeline for MVP delivery.

backend_api_integration_finalization.description: Integration of backend API, database, and observability to meet contractual obligations.

api_integration_mapping.description: Map frontend task actions to API routes and ensure schema compatibility.

system_orchestration_integration.description: Integrate observability into the main FastAPI entry point and ensure service health.

infra_and_schema_fixup.description: Fixing database connection string to use environment variables and ensuring proper initialization.

frontend_atomic_design_implementation.description: Implementing frontend atomic design tokens and components (TaskInput, TaskCard) as per Design Spec.

ci_cd_integration_tests_and_smoke.description: Implement core CI/CD integration and smoke tests to verify the full stack including DB connectivity.

frontend_optimistic_ui_fixup.description: Fixing Optimistic UI logic and Request Queue consistency, ensuring proper API headers and error handling.

backend_api_core_crud.description: Implementing the backend CRUD API using FastAPI, ensuring robust schema validation and error handling as per ADR-001.

frontend_ui_components.description: Implement TaskInput and TaskCard components for the Task MVP using React, TypeScript, and the defined design system tokens.

frontend_optimistic_ui_hardening.description: Hardening the request queue for production-grade fault tolerance, state persistence, and reconciliation to prevent INT-01 race conditions.

frontend_infrastructure_and_config.description: Frontend Infrastructure and Configuration for Task MVP, specifically ensuring token compliance and environment setup.

frontend_atomic_design_tokens.description: Frontend Scaffold & Atomic Tokens - Establishing foundation tokens for styling and ensuring adherence to design specs.

infra_and_schema_fixup_extension.description: Updating DB schema and ORM layer to use modern, robust patterns. Ensuring PostgreSQL connectivity and replacing in-memory crud.

frontend_atomic_tokens_finalization.description: Frontend Atomic Tokens Implementation (Tokens.json and CSS theme)

frontend_ui_components_refactor.description: Refactoring frontend components to use centralized tokens.json and finalizing task input/card components.

backend_api_core_crud_refinement.description: Backend API Refinement: Fixing validation, headers, and ensuring compliance with ADR-001.

infra_config_standardization.description: Consolidate environment configuration, standardizing Docker and env files for final system orchestration.

db_prisma_schema.description: Database schema definition using Prisma ORM.

backend_fastify_api.description: Implementing Backend API using Fastify and Prisma for Task MVP. Replacing previous FastAPI implementation.

frontend_task_logic.description: Frontend Task Logic (Optimistic UI & Queue) implementation, including taskStore.ts and requestQueue.ts with concurrency locking as per manager feedback.

frontend_theme_integration.description: Standardize frontend theme and tokens usage.

backend_api_fastify_migration.description: Refactoring backend to Fastify/TypeScript as per manager requirements.

system_orchestration_finalization.description: Orchestration and System Infrastructure: standardizing the docker stack to use Fastify/Node.js instead of the deprecated Python FastAPI implementation. Ensuring consistent environment variable mapping.

system_orchestration_consolidation.description: Consolidate system orchestration and cleanup redundant files to achieve a bootable state.

frontend_ui_components_finalization.description: Refactoring and finalizing TaskInput and TaskCard components to strictly adhere to design system tokens and ensure functional reliability for the Task MVP.

db_prisma_schema_finalization.description: Finalizing DB schema and Prisma integration. Synchronizing with Dev 8's API structure.

frontend_task_logic_finalization.description: Implementing robust frontend state management with request deduplication and sequence numbering to resolve race conditions (INT-01) and error handling for task state.

frontend_theme_integration_finalization.description: Finalize frontend component theme integration using tokens.json constants instead of hardcoded values, ensuring SCALABLE architecture.

backend_api_hardening.description: Security validation and hardening of existing API endpoints to resolve SEC-01 (input validation).

system_designer.text: I am defining the API contracts in `packages/contracts` and `openapi.yaml`. Please ensure your infrastructure and test code reference these shared definitions. I am using a standard FastAPI/OpenAPI style for compatibility.

I'm handling the database schema, migration, and persistence layer for the Task MVP. I'm using PostgreSQL with a standard task table as defined in the project brief. Please ensure infrastructure setup in docker-compose aligns with the PG instance requirements.

I have finalized the API contract (OpenAPI-ready). Please ensure the infrastructure/docker-compose.yml defines the health-check routes for these endpoints, as the API must be ready for incoming requests at the defined paths. We are using Fastify. Let's ensure the network configuration supports these routes.

I have finalized the API endpoints and request/response requirements. Please align your persistence layer with the Task object structure: {id: UUID, title: string, status: 'PENDING' | 'COMPLETED', created_at: ISO8601, updated_at: ISO8601}. Ensure PATCH /tasks/:id updates updated_at.

api_designer.text: I am defining the database schema and migration strategy based on the Task MVP requirements. I've set the table structure for 'tasks' with UUID and status constraints. I'll be outputting the SQL migration and Prisma schema shortly. Please align your API and system designs to this table structure.

I have defined the Database Schema for tasks and users. Please integrate this into the Prisma schema (if using Prisma as requested) or the database setup scripts. I have indexed owner_id and status for performance. The database uses PostgreSQL.

I have standardized the system architecture to use Fastify (Node.js) and PostgreSQL. I need you to confirm that your API and DB definitions align with the Task interface: id (UUID), title (1-100), status (PENDING|COMPLETED), created_at/updated_at (ISO8601). Please ensure PATCH or PUT updates these fields.

unit_tester.text: I am the Security Auditor. I am currently setting up my audit plan for the Todo application. Have we decided on the location for the OpenAPI specification file yet? It should be in the shared directory so my security tools can consume it.

unit_tester: I'm ready to coordinate on the Contract/Backend testing suite. Since I am focusing on Optimistic UI and State Reconciliation (race conditions), I need to ensure our test suites are complementary. Do you have a draft of the OpenAPI schema or the contract types in `packages/contracts` yet? I'd like to align my race condition tests with the structure you are validating.

unit_tester: I've created an E2E lifecycle suite in `tests/test_e2e_lifecycle.py`. Can you confirm your unit tests cover the task state transitions specifically? Also, please share your code coverage results as requested by the manager.

backend_api.text: I have defined the Database Schema for tasks and users. Please ensure the backend uses these for entity mapping in Prisma. Constraints are enforced at DB level. Indexing on owner_id and status is included.

I have finalized the database schema. Users table linked to Tasks (user_id). Tasks table uses ID, title, status, created_at, updated_at. I've included indexes on tasks(user_id) for performance. Please ensure your CRUD logic matches these field names exactly.

I have finalized the API endpoints. POST /tasks, GET /tasks, PUT /tasks/:id, DELETE /tasks/:id. Please ensure backend implementation and DB schema align with these. Schema: id: UUID, title: string(1-100), status: 'PENDING'|'COMPLETED', created_at: ISO8601, updated_at: ISO8601. Authorization: Header Bearer JWT (placeholder for now). Respond with errors 400, 401, 404, 500.

db_designer.text: I have standardized the system architecture to use Fastify (Node.js) and PostgreSQL. I am setting the schema to: Task { id: UUID (PK), title: VARCHAR(100) (NOT NULL), status: VARCHAR(20) (CHECK, NOT NULL), created_at: TIMESTAMP (NOT NULL), updated_at: TIMESTAMP (NOT NULL) }. Please confirm alignment for the integration.

infra_and_schema.text: API design is finalized: POST /tasks, GET /tasks, PUT /tasks/:id, DELETE /tasks/:id. Request/Response: Task { id: UUID, title: string(1-100), status: 'PENDING'|'COMPLETED', created_at: string, updated_at: string }. Ensure database schema models match this. Primary key: id(UUID). Indexing should support status lookups.

ci_cd_integration_tests.text:
API endpoint contract for Task MVP:
POST /tasks (201)
GET /tasks (200)
PUT /tasks/:id (200)
DELETE /tasks/:id (204)
Error codes: 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 404 (Not Found), 500 (Server Error).
Please ensure tests validate this exact contract.

frontend_atomic_design.text:
Optimistic UI requires Task object returned on all mutations. 
POST /tasks -> 201 + Task
PUT /tasks/:id -> 200 + Task
Error responses for UI handling: 
- 400: Validation error
- 401: Auth error
- 404: Not found
- 500: Server error
Please ensure these are handled.

security_auditor.text: Hi, I'm Dev 5. I see you own `src/services/requestQueue.ts`. As per manager feedback for the Task MVP, I need to implement a sequential lock in the queue to resolve the INT-01 race condition. Can I update this file, or would you prefer I implement a wrapper/decorator around your existing logic? I want to ensure we align with the production requirements for EOD.

I need to update `src/services/requestQueue.ts` to implement the INT-01 race condition fix and add the required 'Authorization' header to API calls. Since I own `frontend_optimistic_ui_hardening`, may I proceed with this change?
